title: Electronics assembly
abstract: A power electronics assembly for an electric motor controller is disclosed herein. The power electronics assembly comprises a first circuit board,a second circuit board spaced from the first circuit board by a stand-off distance,an electrically insulating housing held between the first circuit board and the second circuit board, a plurality of conductors, and a restraint adapted to hold the electrically insulating housing to the first circuit. The electrically insulating housing comprises a plurality of channels. The plurality of conductors are each arranged to provide a conduction path through a corresponding one of the plurality of channels and are each trapped inbetween the electrically insulating housing and the first circuit board and biased into electrical contact with an electrical conductor of the first circuit board.
claims:
Claims 1. A power electronics assembly for an electric motor controller comprising: a first circuit board; a second circuit board spaced from the first circuit board by a stand-off distance; an electrically insulating housing held between the first circuit board and the second circuit board, wherein the electrically insulating housing comprises a plurality of channels; a plurality of conductors each arranged to provide a conduction path through a corresponding one of the plurality of channels and each trapped inbetween the electrically insulating housing and the first circuit board and biased into electrical contact with an electrical conductor of the first circuit board; a restraint adapted to hold the electrically insulating housing to the first circuit board against a force on the electrically insulating housing caused by the biasing of the plurality of conductors; wherein the second circuit board is held to the first circuit board by a fixture such that the second circuit board presses the conductors to reduce the force on the electrically insulating housing caused by the biasing of the plurality of conductors. 2. The assembly of claim 1 wherein the first circuit board is arranged at a first side of the housing to close the channels thereby to hold the conductors in the channels. 3. The assembly of claim 1 or 2 wherein the channels are arranged to retain the conductors in the event that the second circuit board is unfixed from the first circuit board. 4. The assembly of any preceding claim comprising bias providers arranged to provide force in a bias direction transverse to the first circuit board, wherein the restraint is adapted to hold the electrically insulating housing at a selected location on the first circuit board. 5. The assembly of claim 4 comprising a rigid locator arranged to locate the electrically insulating housing at the selected location and to allow movement of the 

 electrically insulating housing in the bias direction. 6. The assembly of claim 5 wherein the restraint comprises a clip having flexible legs adapted to be deflected transverse to the bias direction, for example wherein the flexible legs are resilient. 7. The assembly of any preceding claim wherein the restraint is electrically insulating. 8. The assembly of any preceding claim wherein the electrically insulating housing is arranged so that the plurality of conductors maintain an electrical connection between the first circuit board and the second circuit board in the event that the second circuit board moves relative to the first circuit board and the electrically insulating housing. 9. The assembly of any preceding claim wherein the restraint comprises a clip for detachably coupling the electrically insulating housing to the first circuit board. 10. The assembly of any preceding claim wherein the electrically insulating housing comprises the restraint. 1 1. An electrical coupling assembly for coupling a first circuit board to a second circuit board of an electric motor controller, the assembly comprising: an electrically insulating housing; a plurality of channels through the electrically insulating housing, wherein each channel comprises an open end and a retaining member; a plurality of conductors each arranged in a corresponding one of the plurality of channels and seated against the retaining member of said corresponding one of the plurality of channels and arranged to provide resilient movement of at least part of said conductor in a bias direction along the channel; and a restraint adapted to be at least partially deflected transverse to the bias direction to secure the electrically insulating housing to the first circuit board thereby to close the open end of the channels to trap the conductors between the retaining members and the first circuit board and to bias the conductors into electrical contact with an electrical 

 conductor of the first circuit board. 12. An electrical coupling assembly for coupling a first circuit board to a second circuit board of an electric motor controller, the assembly comprising: an electrically insulating housing; a plurality of channels through the electrically insulating housing, wherein each channel comprises an open end and a retaining member; a plurality of conductors each arranged in a corresponding one of the plurality of channels and seated against the retaining member of said corresponding one of the plurality of channels and arranged to provide resilient movement of at least part of said conductor in a bias direction along the channel; and a restraint adapted to mechanically secure the electrically insulating housing to the first circuit board via an interference fit thereby to close the open end of the channels to trap the conductors between the retaining members and the first circuit board and to bias the conductors into electrical contact with an electrical conductor of the first circuit board. 13. The assembly of claim 1 1 or 12 wherein each channel terminates in a first aperture on the first side of the electrically insulating housing and a second aperture on a second side of the electrically insulating housing, and each conductor comprises a bias provider configured to project the corresponding one of the conductors through a corresponding one of the first plurality of apertures and through a corresponding one of the second plurality of apertures for electrically coupling the first circuit board with a second circuit board. 14. The assembly of claim 13 wherein the assembly is configured to retain each bias provider between each corresponding retaining member and the first circuit board when, in use, the electrically insulating housing is coupled to the first circuit board by the restraint. 15 The assembly of claim 14 wherein each bias provider is configured to press each corresponding conductor against a corresponding contact on at least one of the circuit boards to maintain an electrical connection between the first circuit board and the second 

 circuit board. 16. The assembly of any of claims 1 1 to 15 wherein the restraint is adapted to hold the electrically insulating housing at a selected location on the first circuit board. 5 17. The assembly of claim 16 wherein the restraint comprises a rigid locator arranged to locate the electrically insulating housing at the selected location and to allow movement of the electrically insulating housing in the bias direction. 10 18. An electrical coupling assembly for coupling a first circuit board to a second circuit board of an electric motor controller, the assembly comprising: an electrically insulating housing; a plurality of channels through the electrically insulating housing, wherein each channel comprises an open end and a retaining member; 15 a plurality of conductors each arranged in a corresponding one of the plurality of channels and seated against the retaining member of said corresponding one of the plurality of channels and arranged to provide resilient movement of at least part of said conductor in a bias direction along the channel; a restraint adapted to engage and mechanically secure the electrically insulating 20 housing to the first circuit board in the bias direction thereby to close the open end of the channels to trap the conductors between the retaining members and the first circuit board and to bias the conductors into electrical contact with an electrical conductor of the first circuit board; and a rigid locator adapted to align the electrically insulating housing with a location on 25 the first circuit board prior to engagement of the restraint with the first circuit board and to permit movement of the electrically insulating housing in the bias direction. 19. The assembly of claim 17 or 18 wherein the restraint comprises at least one flexible leg adapted to be deflected transverse to the bias direction relative to the rigid 30 locator to allow the electrically insulating housing to be locked in position at the selected location. 20. The assembly of claim 19 wherein the rigid locator is arranged to inhibit lateral 

 movement of the electrically insulating housing across the first circuit board, and the flexible legs are arranged to hold the electrically insulating housing against the first circuit board in opposition to the force applied to the electrically insulating housing by the biasing of the plurality of conductors. 5 21. The assembly of claim 19 or 20 wherein the rigid locator is adapted to oppose a force transverse to the bias direction on the electrically insulating housing caused by interaction between the restraint and the first circuit board. 10 22. The assembly of any of claims 17 to 21 wherein the rigid locator extends further from the housing in the bias direction than does the at least one flexible leg. 23. The assembly of any of claims 17 to 22 wherein the at least one flexible leg comprises a flange adapted to lock the housing to a circuit board, and a ramp adapted to 15 deflect the at least one flexible leg. 24. The assembly of claim 23 wherein the restraint comprises at least two legs, the legs each adapted to snap-fit with a corresponding hole in the first circuit board. 20 25. The assembly of any of claims 17 to 24 wherein the rigid locator comprises at least one locator pin for aligning the electrically insulating housing at a selected location on the first circuit board. 26. The assembly of any of the previous claims wherein each conductor comprises a 25 bias provider, such as a spring, configured to press each corresponding conductor against a corresponding contact on at least one of the circuit boards along an axis corresponding to a longitudinal axis of each channel. 27. The assembly of any of the previous claims wherein each bias provider comprises 30 the corresponding conductor, for example wherein the conductors are provided by springs. 28. The assembly of any of the previous claims wherein each conductor comprises at 

 least one pin to engage a contact on at least one of the circuit boards. 29. The assembly of any of the previous claims wherein each conductor comprises a coil or helix structure that varies in diameter over a portion of its length. 5 30. The assembly of claim 29 wherein the portion that varies in diameter is at an end of the conductor. 31. The assembly of claim 29 or 30 wherein the portion of each conductor that varies 10 in diameter is one of an increase and a decrease in diameter. 32. The assembly of any of the previous claims wherein the electrically insulating housing is configured to align each corresponding conductor with a corresponding electrically conductive pad on the first circuit board and a corresponding electrically 15 conductive pad on the second circuit board. 33. The assembly of any of the previous claims wherein each of the plurality of channels and corresponding first and second apertures are equally spaced. 20 34. The assembly of any of the previous claims wherein each conductor is configured to project through a corresponding one of a first plurality of apertures of the electrically insulating housing. 35. The assembly of claim 34 wherein the electrically insulating housing is configured 25 to retain a portion of each conductor between the retaining member and the first circuit board. 36. The assembly of claim 34 or 35 wherein the electrically insulating housing is configured to retain a portion of each bias provider between the retaining member and 30 the first circuit board. 37. The assembly of any preceding claim wherein the retaining members each comprises a flange. 38. The assembly of any preceding claim wherein the channels comprise a narrow region adapted to provide said retaining member. 5 39. The assembly of claim 38 wherein a portion of the conductor and/or bias provider is adapted to fit through the narrow region. 40. The assembly of any of the previous claims wherein the second circuit board comprises an insulated metal substrate, with a printed circuit board printed over the 10 insulated metal substrate. 41. The assembly of claim 40 wherein the insulated metal substrate is coupled to a heat sink. 15 42. The assembly of any of the previous claims comprising a plurality of electrically insulating housings and corresponding conductors. 43. An electric motor controller comprising an assembly according to any of the previous claims. 20 44. A method of manufacturing a power electronics assembly, the method comprising: providing an electrically insulating housing comprising a plurality of channels, wherein each channel comprises a retaining member; 25 arranging a conductor in each channel each a plurality of conductors is arranged to provide a conduction path through a corresponding one of the plurality of channels and so that, in each channel, a bias provider is seated against a retaining member and arranged to permit resilient movement of the each one of the plurality of conductors along the each channel; 30 coupling the electrically insulating housing to a first circuit board to enclose the plurality of conductors in the plurality of channels so that the bias providers urge the conductors into electrical contact with the first circuit board; fixing a second circuit board to the second circuit board to bring the 

 second circuit board into electrical contact with the plurality conductors and to further urge the plurality of conductors into electrical contact with the first circuit board. 5 45. The method of claim 44, wherein each channel terminates in a first aperture on a first side of the electrically insulating housing and a second aperture on a second side of the electrically insulating housing, the method comprising inserting a conductors through each first aperture into each channel to engage each corresponding retaining member, and closing the first apertures with the first circuit board. 10 46. The method of claim 45 wherein closing the first apertures comprises trapping the bias providers within each corresponding channel of the electrically insulating housing between each corresponding retaining member and the first circuit board. 15 47. The method of claim 44, 45 or 46 further comprising mechanically coupling the first circuit board to a second circuit board using a fixture, such as a bolt, so that the second side of the electrically insulating housing faces the second circuit board, and each of the conductors electrically engages with a corresponding contact on the first circuit board and on the second circuit board to electrically couple the first circuit board to 20 the second circuit board. 48. The method of claim 47 wherein the electrically insulating housing further comprises a clip, and coupling the electrically insulating housing to the first circuit board comprises pressing the electrically insulating housing against the first circuit board in a 25 direction parallel to the longitudinal axis of each of the channels, and further comprises moving a portion of the clip in a direction transverse to the longitudinal axis of each of the channels as the electrically insulating housing is pressed against the first circuit board. 49. The method of any of claims 44 to 48 wherein the electrically insulating housing 30 further comprises a rigid locator, and coupling the electrically insulating housing to the first circuit board comprises aligning the rigid locator with a corresponding hole on the first circuit board, and pressing the electrically insulating housing against the first circuit board so that the rigid locator slides within the corresponding hole in the first circuit 

 board. 50. An assembly substantially as described herein with reference to the drawings. 51. A method of manufacture substantially as described herein with reference to the drawings.
